Smyth was then proprictor and editor, a description of this engmnng,
with a copy of the verses, appeared on the 19th September, 1863.

The verses were published in ““The Irish Packet’’ (1903), and in other
papers. They also appeared in ‘‘ The Christian Brothers’ Magazine ™
(1907), with the heading * Lmes written by William Smith O’Brien—
now puh];c;hcd for the first time.’

W. S. O'Brien—who was 1n the habit of writing poetry—it will be of
interest to many to bring to light the following letter addressed by him
to Edmund Hayes :(—

THE 1IRISH BOOK LOYER. 28
FORTHCOMING WORKS.

Mr. W. J. Laurence of Dublin is busy on a series of biographies
of actors and actresses ior the supplement to the Dictionary of National
Biography. He is also about to publish with Mr. A. H. Bullen of the
Shakespeare Head Press, a volume entitled, ‘‘ An Elizabethan Play-
house and other Studies.’’

Mr. D. J. O’Donoghue is busily engaged in completing the new
edition of his ‘‘ Poets of Ireland.” Of this most valuable work only
two parts have as yet been issued, and, like everyone else who requires
such a useful work of reference, we shall gladly welcome the forth-
coming parts.

That veteran and indefatigable author, Mr. Justin McCarthy, has
written another volume of his ‘‘ Irish Reminiscences,” which Hodder
and Stoughton will publish during the autumn. :

In fiction, Messrs. Methuen will issue in the autumn two new works, -
* Lalage’s Lovers,”” by George Birmingham, which has been runiing as
a serial, and ‘“ Dan Russel the Fox,” by the Misses Somerville and Ross.

They also announce a2 shtllmrr edition of the first-named authors"
humorous novel, * bpamch Gold.”
